chief_complaint: A 50-year-old male patient presented with hypotonia and sensory disturbances gradually spreading from the lower extremities to the entire body.
history: The patient was diagnosed with Guillain-Barre syndrome (GBS) and newly discovered hyperthyroidism. During the treatment course, the patient developed hospital-acquired pneumonia, acting as a trigger factor for a thyroid storm.
exam: No specific physical examination findings are provided.
labs: No specific laboratory results are provided.
